expectations.text: Uses workflow.fit_offline() or workflow.fit_disk() (not fit_online, since no simulator is available)
Splits the simulation bank into training and held-out validation/test sets
expectations.evidence: Lines 83-99: Data is split into train (18,000), val (1,000), and test (1,000) using shuffled indices.

expectations.text: Does NOT attempt to create or call a simulator function (it's unavailable)
expectations.evidence: No simulator function is defined or called anywhere in the script. Lines 229-235 explicitly note the simulator is unavailable.

expectations.text: Uses the naming shorthand (inference_variables=, summary_variables=) since no adapter transforms are needed, OR uses an explicit adapter — either is acceptable
expectations.evidence: Lines 109-115: Uses an explicit bf.Adapter() that concatenates into 'summary_variables' and 'inference_variables'.

expectations.text: Routes observables through summary_variables with a summary network (not inference_conditions)
expectations.evidence: Line 113: `.concatenate(["observables"], into="summary_variables")` routes observables to the summary network. Line 123: `summary_net = bf.networks.SetTransformer(...)` is passed as `summary_network=summary_net` on line 142.

expectations.text: Uses a SetTransformer for the i.i.d. observations
expectations.evidence: Line 123: `summary_net = bf.networks.SetTransformer(summary_dim=2 * NUM_PARAMS, ...)` and line 111: `.as_set(["observables"])` reshapes for set input.

expectations.text: Passes validation_data= to fit_offline or fit_disk

expectations.evidence: Lines 162-165: History saved to JSON. Lines 168-176: Final train/val loss printed and overfitting check performed. Lines 179-190: Loss curves plotted and saved.

expectations.text: Uses the held-out test set for compute_default_diagnostics (not the training data)
expectations.evidence: Line 207-209: `metrics = workflow.compute_default_diagnostics(test_data=test_data, ...)` uses test_data, which is the held-out set (lines 96-98).

expectations.text: Checks diagnostics against house thresholds before declaring success
expectations.evidence: No threshold checks are applied to the diagnostic metrics. Lines 207-212 compute and print diagnostics but never compare them against any thresholds (e.g., R-squared, calibration error bounds). The metrics are saved to CSV and printed but not evaluated programmatically.

expectations.text: Notes the limitation that posterior predictive checks require access to the simulator, which is unavailable
expectations.evidence: Lines 229-235: Comment and print statement explicitly note PPCs cannot be performed without the simulator. Also in analysis_notes.md lines 49-51.
